heading: Permissible revisions of concurrent resolutions on the budget
label: 635 Permissible revisions of concurrent resolutions on the budget
text: At any time after the concurrent resolution on the budget for a fiscal year has been agreed to pursuant to , and before the end of such fiscal year, the two Houses may adopt a concurrent resolution on the budget which revises or reaffirms the concurrent resolution on the budget for such fiscal year most recently agreed to.section 632 of this title
notes: CodificationSection was formerly classified to prior to the general revision and enactment of Title 31, Money and Finance, by , , .section 1325 of Title 31Pub. L. 97–258, § 196 Stat. 877Sept. 13, 1982

Amendments1997— designated subsec. (a) as entire section and struck out subsec. (a) heading “In general” and subsec. (b) heading and text. Prior to amendment, text of subsec. (b) read as follows: “The provisions of shall apply with respect to concurrent resolutions on the budget under this section (and amendments thereto and conference reports thereon) in the same way they apply to concurrent resolutions on the budget under such (and amendments thereto and conference reports thereon).”Pub. L. 105–33section 632(g) of this titlesection 632(g) of this title1990—Subsecs. (b), (c). redesignated subsec. (c) as (b) and struck out former subsec. (b) which read as follows: “The provisions of shall apply with respect to concurrent resolutions on the budget under this section (and amendments thereto and conference reports thereon) in the same way they apply to concurrent resolutions on the budget under such (and amendments thereto and conference reports thereon).”Pub. L. 101–508section 632(i) of this titlesection 632(i) of this title1987—Subsec. (c). added subsec. (c).Pub. L. 100–1191985—, in amending section generally, inserted “Permissible” before “revisions” in section catchline, designated existing provisions as subsec. (a), struck out “first” after “after the”, and added subsec. (b).Pub. L. 99–177

Effective Date of 1985 AmendmentAmendment by effective , and applicable with respect to fiscal years beginning after , see , formerly set out as an Effective and Termination Dates note under prior to repeal by , , .Pub.
